title: Papr: Use updates-testing repo for packages
body:
This supports podman-in-podman testing on Fedora with a newer version of
podman on the host-side.  This is needed to validate changes/fixes
before they are released generally.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: @rhatdan I think this is what you were asking for, was it something more?

Fingers crossed for CI

Though papr isn't even running

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Plan B here is:  update ``.papr.sh`` to enable updates-testing repo, and ``yum reinstall -y podman``.
it's not re-triggering.  Rearranging the commits and --force pushing.
/me shakes his fist at papr: Chooch!
Those openshift failures seem like mechanical flakes, not test failures.  But I'm not the expert.
